npcs.name: Big Sorrel
npcs.title: who keeps the ring
npcs.hail: *(a vast, grinning shade with a voice built for crowds stands at the head of a ring of fire-lit logs, banging a barrel-head for a drum, and the instant your cold falls across the circle it bellows a welcome that turns every shade present to look at you — because Big Sorrel has run this ring for longer than it can remember, and it can always, always smell a soul with a story it hasn't heard yet)*

npcs.name: the Reigning Tongue
npcs.title: undefeated, and tired of it
npcs.hail: *(a lean shade lounges on the best log with the studied ease of a champion, a circlet of woven reeds on its brow marking it the ring's reigning boaster — but up close the ease is a performance, and what's under it is the specific exhaustion of a soul that has won every round for so long it has run clean out of true things to say and started, lately, to lie)*

npcs.name: the Smallest Voice
npcs.title: who has no story to tell
npcs.hail: *(a faint, fraying shade hovers at the very edge of the firelight, watching the boasting with naked longing and naked terror both, because it has worked out the ring's secret — that a soul who can tell a grand true story of its life is a soul too vivid to be forgotten — and it has also worked out, with despair, that it has no grand story, only a small ordinary life, and so it is fading, fastest of anyone here, for want of a boast)*

variants.text: *(The vast shade bangs its barrel-drum and throws an arm wide, and the whole fire-lit ring turns your way.)* …*WELL* now! *(a crowd-filling bellow, delighted)* *Cold* one walks into the *ring*! *(it grins enormous)* You'll *forgive* the *fuss*, friend — we *don't* get many *living-cold* down here, and a *new* soul in the *Boasting-Ring* is a *new* *story*, and a *new* story is the *one* thing the *dead* still *hunger* for that *isn't* *cruel.* *(it bangs the drum)* *Big* *Sorrel*, friend! I *keep* the *ring!* The *rules* are *simple* — you *take* the *log*, you *tell* the *grandest* *TRUE* thing about your *life*, and the *ring* *judges* who told it *best.* *(it leans in, suddenly quieter, almost confiding)* And here's the *thing* the *newcomers* never *guess*, cold one: it's not a *game.* Or — it *is*, but it's a *game* that *KEEPS* us. *(it taps its own chest)* A soul that can *tell* a *grand* *true* *story* of *who* it *was* is a soul too *VIVID* to be *forgotten.* The *boasting* is how we *refuse* the *Wall*, friend. We *out-loud* ourselves so the *Hunger* can't *un-make* us *quiet.* *(a grin)* So! *Will* you *take* the *log?* A *cold* one's *story* would be a *RARE* round indeed!

nodes.speaker: Big Sorrel
nodes.text: *(It settles its bulk onto the head-log, drum across its knees, beaming at you.)* So! *(it booms)* What'll it *be*, cold one? *Questions* about the *ring*, or are you *takin'* the *log?* I'll *warn* you — the *Reigning* *Tongue's* not *lost* a *round* in an *age*, and the *little* *one* at the *edge* there's *fadin'* for *want* of a *story*, and *somewhere* in *all* that there's a *night* worth *havin'.* *Speak* up! The *ring* *listens!*

choices.text: "A game that keeps you — say more, Sorrel. How does boasting hold back the Wall?"

*(take the log and tell your own grand true story)* "I'll take the log. Here's my true thing: I died. The harvest took me, the cold closed over, the Wall claimed me — and I walked back OUT, the one soul in the after that did, and I've spent every step since pulling other souls out of the cold behind me. Beat THAT, ring."
"The little one at the edge — the fading one. Tell me about it before I do anything else."

*(the cold soul names what the ring really is)* "I see what you've built here, Sorrel — and it's cleverer than a game. Every soul down here is starving to be witnessed, and you made a ring where they witness each *other*, out loud, in turns. You're not running a contest. You're running a machine that keeps the forgotten from fading, one story at a time. That's the same thing I've been doing the whole causeway. We're in the same trade."
"Maybe later, Sorrel. Let me look around the ring first."

nodes.speaker: Big Sorrel
nodes.text: *(The booming drops to something quieter and realer, the showman setting down the show.)* …How it *holds* back the *Wall.* *(it taps the drum, soft)* You've *seen* it, cold one — you've *walked* the *causeway*, I can *tell* — you've *seen* what *happens* to the *forgotten.* They *fade.* They *thin.* The *Hunger* doesn't *come* for the *loud* and the *loved* *first* — it comes for the *quiet* ones, the *un-witnessed*, the souls *no* one *remembers* *hard* enough to *hold* in the *world.* *(it gestures at the ring of listening shades)* So I *built* this. A *ring* where every soul *takes* a *turn* being *witnessed* — *loudly*, *gloriously*, by *everyone* at *once.* *(it grins)* We *call* it a *contest*, because a *contest's* *fun*, and *fun* gets souls to *come.* But the *real* prize isn't the *reed-crown.* It's that for the *length* of your *boast*, *forty* souls are *looking* *right* *at* *you*, *holding* you *vivid*, *refusing* to *let* you *fade.* *(softly)* *Every* soul that *takes* the *log* walks *away* a *little* *more* *real* than it *sat* *down.* *That's* the *game*, cold one. The *winning's* *nothing.* The *being-witnessed* is *everything.* It's the *cheapest*, *loudest*, *drunkest* *defiance* of the *Wall* there *is* — and it *WORKS*, which is more than I can say for *prayer.*

nodes.speaker: Big Sorrel
nodes.text: *(It listens — the whole ring listens — and when you finish there is a silence, and then Big Sorrel comes off the head-log and the barrel-drum thunders and the ring ERUPTS.)* …*THE* *RETURNED!* *(it bellows, ecstatic)* The soul that *walked* *OUT* of the *WALL!* *(the shades are on their feet)* Cold one, in *all* the *years* I've *kept* this *ring*, I have *never* — *NEVER* — heard a *truer* *thing* told *grander*, and I have heard *KINGS* *boast!* *(it seizes your hand and hauls it up)* You didn't just *win* the *round*, friend. You *broke* the *ring* — because *every* soul *here* just *witnessed* the *one* story that *proves* the *Wall* can be *BEATEN*, and *that* story, *told* in *this* *ring*, will be *re-told* by *forty* souls to *forty* *more*, and *spread* — *(it's almost weeping with delight)* — do you *understand*, cold one? You've made yourself the *most* *un-forgettable* soul on the *causeway.* The *Hunger* will *NEVER* *get* you now. *Forty* witnesses *tonight*, *four* *hundred* by *next* *moon*, your *story* *passed* *soul-to-soul* like a *coal* that *won't* *go* *out.* *(it claps your shoulder, beaming)* *THAT'S* a *boast.* *Sit.* *Drink.* You've *earned* the *best* *log* in the *ring* — and you've *given* every *fading* soul *here* the *grandest* *gift* the *ring* has: *proof* that a *story* *loud* *enough* can *outlast* *anything.* *Even* *death.* *Even* the *Wall.*

nodes.speaker: Big Sorrel
nodes.text: *(The grin fades, and the showman's face goes gentle and worried.)* …*Ah.* The *Smallest* *Voice.* *(it lowers the drum)* That one *breaks* my *old* *heart*, cold one, and I *don't* *break* *easy.* *(it watches the fraying shade at the edge)* Most who *come* to the *ring* have a *story* in *them* somewhere — you just *coax* it *out*, *prime* the *pump*, and they *boast* and they *brighten.* *(it shakes its head)* But *that* one. It's *got* — it *thinks* it's *got* — *nothing.* No *grand* *deed*, no *great* *love*, no *dragon* *slain* or *fortune* *won.* Just a *small*, *quiet*, *ordinary* *life*, lived *gentle* and *un-noticed*, and *now* it's *FADING* for want of a *boast* it *doesn't* *think* it *has.* *(it meets your eyes)* And the *cruel* *joke*, cold one — the thing I *can't* *fix*, much as I *bellow* — is that the *ring* *rewards* the *LOUD* *story*, the *grand* one, and a *gentle* *ordinary* life *doesn't* *boast* *easy*, even though — *(it struggles)* — even though I *KNOW*, in my *old* *bones*, that a *gentle* *ordinary* life *well-lived* is *worth* *ten* *dragon-slayings*, if only a soul knew *how* to *TELL* it. *(it looks at you)* *You're* *clever*, cold one. *Cold* and *clever.* If *anyone* could find the *boast* *hidden* in a *life* that *thinks* it has *none* — *help* *that* one. *Before* it *fades.* I'd *count* it the *finest* round the *ring* ever *held*, and *no* *crown* *given.*

nodes.speaker: Big Sorrel
nodes.text: *(The vast shade goes still, and then sets the drum down entirely, and looks at you with a wondering respect that the booming showman rarely shows.)* …*The* *same* *trade.* *(quietly)* *Huh.* *(it rubs its jaw)* You know, cold one, in *all* the *years* I've *kept* this *ring*, *bellowing* and *drumming* and *playing* the *fool* *ringmaster*, *nobody* has *ever* *seen* *past* the *show* to the *thing* under it. *(it almost smiles)* They *think* I'm a *loud* *old* soul who *likes* an *audience.* And I *AM.* But you're *right* — *underneath*, I'm — *(it searches for the word you handed it)* — I'm *running* a *machine* that *keeps* the *forgotten* from *fading.* *(it looks at the ring of shades)* I *started* it for *me*, if I'm *honest. I* was *fading*, *once*, *long* ago — *loud* in *life*, *forgotten* in *death*, the *worst* *fall* there *is* — and I *found* that if I *got* souls *telling* *stories*, and *listening* to *each* *other's*, *none* of *us* *faded* so *fast.* *(it meets your eyes)* So *yes*, cold one. *Same* *trade.* You *walk* the *causeway* *witnessing* souls *one* at a *time*; I *built* a *ring* where they *witness* *each* *other* *forty* at *once.* *(it grins, and there's a partnership in it)* You're the *first* soul to *call* it what it *is.* So *here's* my *offer*, *fellow* *witness*: *take* the *log*, *tell* your *grand* *true* *thing* — and then *help* me with the *little* *fading* one, because *between* a *soul* that *witnesses* *one-by-one* and a *ring* that *witnesses* *forty-at-once*, we might just *keep* the *Smallest* *Voice* from *going* *out.* *Deal?* *Witness* to *witness?*

nodes.speaker: Big Sorrel
nodes.text: *(It sweeps an arm toward the ring of logs, undimmed.)* *Look* all you *like*, cold one! The *ring's* *open*, the *fire's* *warm*, and the *stories* are *FREE* — well, *free* to *hear.* *Tellin's* got a *price*, but the *price* is just *nerve*, and you've the *look* of a soul that's *got* some. *(it bangs the drum once, cheerful)* *Mingle!* *Hear* the *Reigning* *Tongue* — *undefeated*, *though* between *us* it's *started* *lyin'*, and a *lie* in the *Boasting-Ring* is a *sad* *thing*, the *Wall* *catches* up *quick* to a soul that *boasts* *false.* *(quieter)* And *mind* the *little* one at the *edge.* *(it nods at the fading shade)* The *ring* *can't* *save* *every* soul, cold one. But it *hates* to *lose* one for *want* of a *story.* *Hates* it. *Go* on. *See* what the *night* *offers.* And if a *boast* *rises* in you — *(a grin)* — the *log's* *always* *open.* The *ring* is *never* *full.*

variants.text: *(The lean shade adjusts its reed-crown and looks you over with the appraising eye of a champion sizing up a challenger, but the swagger is a beat too slow, too practiced.)* …A *new* tongue in the *ring.* *(a thin smile)* *Cold* one, no less. *(it lounges back)* They'll *want* you to *challenge* me, you know. The *crowd.* *Big* *Sorrel.* *'New* soul, *take* the *log*, *unseat* the *Reigning* *Tongue!'* *(it waves a languid hand)* I've *worn* this *reed-crown* longer than *anyone* in the *ring's* *memory.* *Undefeated.* *Three* *hundred* *boasts*, *three* *hundred* *wins.* *(and here the swagger thins, just slightly)* …Which is, between you and me, cold one, the *worst* *thing* that's *ever* *happened* to me. But you didn't *come* to hear a *champion* *complain.* *(it rallies the smile)* You *came*, I *expect*, to *take* my *crown.* *(a pause)* *Everyone* does. *Eventually.* I *almost* *wish* one of them *would.*

nodes.speaker: the Reigning Tongue
nodes.text: *(It turns the reed-crown in its fingers, the picture of bored mastery, watching you.)* *Speak*, then, cold one. *Challenge*, *question*, *flattery* — I've *heard* them *all*, *three* *hundred* *times* over. *(a thin smile)* *Surprise* me. *Gods*, I'd *love* to be *surprised.*

choices.text: [INSIGHT] "You don't want to win, do you. Three hundred rounds undefeated and you said it like a sentence, not a record. You've run out of true stories — used up your whole life in the first hundred boasts — and now you're lying to keep the crown, and the lying's killing you, because a false boast doesn't hold back the Wall. You're the loudest soul in the ring and you're fading fastest, behind the crown."
"Why does it have to be a contest at all? If the boasting is what keeps you, why crown a winner — why not just let everyone be heard?"

*(offer the champion a way to lose well)* "Here's a thought from a soul that's seen the whole causeway: the crown's not keeping you vivid, it's keeping you *performing*, and performance fades faster than truth. So lose, on purpose, to the next true story — give the crown away. A soul that hands its crown to a truer boast isn't a fallen champion. It's the one who finally understood what the ring was for."
"What was your true story, Tongue? The first one, before the lies. The one that won the crown to begin with."
"Keep your crown, Tongue. I'll not challenge tonight."

nodes.speaker: the Reigning Tongue
nodes.text: *(The reed-crown stops turning. The lounging swagger drains away, and what's left is a soul that has been seen through for the first time in three hundred rounds, and is — beneath the alarm — desperately grateful for it.)* …*Fading* *fastest.* *Behind* the *crown.* *(barely)* You — *(it sets the crown down)* — *how* did you — *no.* You're *right.* That's *how.* You *looked.* *(it presses a hand to its face)* I *used* up my *life* in the *first* *hundred* *boasts*, cold one. *Every* *true* *thing* I ever *did* — *spent*, *told*, *re-told*, *worn* *smooth* and *then* *worn* *THROUGH.* *(its voice cracks)* And the *crown* — *gods*, the *crown* — once you *wear* it, you *can't* *take* it *off*, because *losing* it means *admitting* you've *run* *out*, and *running* *out* means *fading*, so I — I started *embroidering.* *Then* *inventing.* *Then* *flat* *LYING.* *(it laughs, bitter)* The *Reigning* *Tongue*, *undefeated* *champion* of the *Boasting-Ring*, *fading* *faster* than the *humblest* *soul* *here*, because *every* *false* *boast* is a *thing* the *Wall* *KNOWS* *isn't* *me*, and *witnessing* a *LIE* doesn't *hold* a *soul*, it *HOLLOWS* it. *(it looks at you, stripped bare)* I'm *starving* at a *feast* of my *own* *making, cold* one. The *loudest*, *emptiest* soul in the *ring.* *(barely)* …*How* do I *stop?* I've *forgotten* how to *be* *witnessed* for *anything* *TRUE.* I've been *performing* so *long* I don't *know* if there's a *real* *me* *left* to *boast.*

nodes.speaker: the Reigning Tongue
nodes.text: *(It considers the question, and the answer surprises even itself.)* …*Why* a *contest.* *(slowly)* *Big* *Sorrel* would tell you it's because a *contest's* *fun*, gets souls to *come*, and *that's* *true.* *(it turns the crown)* But here's the *uglier* *truth*, cold one, the one *I've* *learned* *wearing* this *thing*: the *contest* is the *FLAW.* *(it meets your eyes)* The *ring's* *meant* to *keep* us *all* *vivid* by *witnessing* *each* *other* — *Sorrel's* *right* about *that.* But the *moment* you make it a *CONTEST*, you make it *SCARCE* — *one* *crown*, *one* *winner*, and *everyone* *else* a *LOSER* — and *scarcity* is the *Wall's* *whole* *GAME.* *(bitter)* The *contest* *helps* the *loud* and *starves* the *quiet*, *crowns* the *boldest* *liar* and *fades* the *humblest* *truth.* *(it looks toward the fading shade at the edge)* *That* one — the *Smallest* *Voice* — it's *dying* *BECAUSE* the *ring* is a *contest* it *can't* *win.* *(quietly)* If the *ring* just *let* *everyone* be *heard*, *no* *crown*, *no* *winner*, *every* *story* *equal* — *nobody* would *fade.* But then *I'd* be *nobody* *special*, and *Sorrel'd* lose his *show*, and — *(it stops)* — *gods*, *listen* to me. The *champion* *arguing* against the *championship.* *(a hollow laugh)* *Maybe* *that's* the *truest* *boast* I've *got* *left*, cold one: I *won* the *thing*, and I *can* *see* it's *rotten*, and I *can't* *put* it *down.* *That's* a *story.* It's just not a *grand* one.

nodes.speaker: the Reigning Tongue
nodes.text: *(It stares at you, and the offer lands like water on a soul that didn't know it was on fire.)* …*Hand* the *crown* *away.* *(barely)* *Lose*, on *purpose.* To a *truer* *story.* *(it turns the reed-crown over and over)* I never — cold one, in *three* *hundred* *rounds* it *never* *once* *occurred* to me that I could *CHOOSE* to *lose.* I thought the *only* *way* *out* of the *crown* was to be *BEATEN* — and I'm too *good*, or too *practiced* at *lying*, to be *beaten* — so I thought I was *STUCK* with it *forever.* *(it looks at you, dawning)* But *giving* it — *choosing* the *truer* *boast* over my *own* — *(its voice strengthens)* — *that's* not *losing*, is it. That's the *one* *move* the *game* *doesn't* *have* a *counter* for. *(it rises, the swagger replaced by something realer)* The *Smallest* *Voice.* The *fading* one. If you can *coax* a *true* *story* out of *it* — *any* *true* *thing*, *however* *small* — I will *stand* up in *this* *ring* and I will *declare* it the *winner*, and I will *put* this *crown* on its *fraying* *brow* *myself*, and the *whole* *ring* will *WITNESS* the *humblest* soul *here* *crowned* over the *champion.* *(fierce, freed)* And *THAT*, cold one — a *champion* who *gave* his *crown* to the *least* of the *ring* — *that's* a *TRUE* *boast*, the *first* I'll have *had* in an *age*, and *it'll* *hold* me *better* than *three* *hundred* *lies* ever did. *Find* the *little* one its *story.* I'll *hand* over the *crown.* *Gods*, what a *RELIEF* it'll be to *lose.*

nodes.speaker: the Reigning Tongue
nodes.text: *(Something softens in it, and for a moment the champion is just a soul remembering when its stories were true.)* …My *first* *story.* *(quietly)* The one that *won* the *crown.* *(it almost smiles)* I was a *bridge-toll* *keeper, cold* one. In *life.* *Dull* as *ditchwater*, you'd *think.* But there was a *flood* — the *worst* in *memory* — and the *bridge* was *going*, and there were *folk* on the *wrong* *side*, and I — *(it straightens, and the old true pride is real)* — I *stood* on that *failing* *bridge* and I *carried* them *across*, *one* by *one*, *through* the *rising* *water*, *forty-three* *souls*, and the *bridge* *went* *under* *moments* after the *last* one, and I *went* *with* it, and *drowned*, and *that's* how I *died*: *carrying* the *forty-third* soul to *safety* as the *river* *took* me. *(it meets your eyes)* *THAT* won the *crown*, cold one. And it *deserved* to. It was *TRUE*, and it was *GRAND*, and *every* time I *told* it the *ring* *roared* and I *blazed* *bright* as the *fire.* *(the light dims)* But you can only *tell* the *same* *true* *story* so *many* *times* before it *wears* *thin*, and I had *only* the *one* — *one* *grand* *thing* in an *otherwise* *ordinary* *life* — and once I'd *worn* it *through*, I had *nothing* *left* but *lies.* *(barely)* I *saved* *forty-three* *souls*, cold one. And it *wasn't* *enough* of a *story* to *last* me. *That's* the *cruelty* of the *ring*: it made me *spend* the *best* *thing* I *ever* *did* until it *meant* *nothing*, and *then* it made me *lie* to *keep* the *applause* the *truth* used to *earn.*

nodes.speaker: the Reigning Tongue
nodes.text: *(It laughs, smooth and practiced, and the crown stays firmly on its brow.)* …*'Run* *out* of *true* *stories.'* *(a languid wave)* *Cold* one, I have *MORE* *true* *stories* than the *ring* has *LOGS.* *(it spreads its hands)* The *time* I *out-drank* a *demon.* The *time* I *charmed* a *Justiciar* out of her *writ.* The *time* I — *(smoothly)* — *no*, you'll *have* to *take* the *log* and *EARN* the *hearing*, like *everyone* *else.* *(a thin smile that doesn't reach its eyes)* You *reach* for a *clever* *read* — *'the* *champion's* *secretly* *fading'* — because it's *easier* than *challenging* me *straight.* *(it settles back)* But I've *worn* this *crown* *three* *hundred* *rounds*, cold one, and I did *not* *do* it by *letting* *clever* *cold* *souls* *psychoanalyze* me out of it. *Take* the *log* or *don't.* But *don't* *think* you've *SEEN* me. *Better* *tongues* than *yours* have *tried.* *(and the swagger holds, but something behind the eyes flinches, just once, at how close you came.)*

nodes.speaker: the Reigning Tongue
nodes.text: *(It inclines its crowned head with practiced grace, relieved and disappointed at once.)* …*No* *challenge.* *(a thin smile)* *Wise*, perhaps. *Or* *kind.* I *can't* *always* *tell* the *difference* anymore, cold one — I've *been* *performing* so *long.* *(it turns the crown)* *Go* on, then. *Mingle.* *Hear* the *others.* *(quieter, as you turn)* …And cold one — if you *do* *take* the *log*, *some* *night* — *tell* a *TRUE* one. *(barely, the mask slipping)* The *ring* *deserves* a *true* one. It's been a *while* since it *had* *one* *from* *this* *log.* *(it touches its crown)* …*Longer* than I'd *like* to *boast.* *Go* on. *Before* I *say* a *true* *thing* by *accident* and *ruin* my *whole* *reputation.*

variants.text: *(The fraying shade startles as your cold reaches it, and shrinks, and then — finding you're not here to mock — steadies into a small, sad honesty.)* …Oh. You're — you're not going to ask me to *boast*, are you. *(barely)* *Everyone* *asks.* *Big* *Sorrel*, kind as he *is*, *'take* the *log*, *little* one, *everyone's* *got* a *story!'* *(it shakes its fraying head)* But I *don't.* That's the — that's the *thing* *nobody* *believes.* I *lived* a *small* *life*, cold one. A *quiet* one. *No* *floods* *survived*, no *demons* *out-drunk*, no *grand* *love* nor *great* *deed.* I was a — *(it almost laughs)* — I *swept* a *temple* *floor.* *Forty* *years.* *That's* — *that's* the *whole* of it. I *swept* a *floor.* *(it's fading visibly, even as you watch)* And the *ring* *rewards* *GRAND* *stories*, and a *swept* *floor* isn't *grand*, so I *can't* *win*, so I *can't* *be* *witnessed*, so I'm — *(barely)* — I'm *going* *out*, cold one. *Fastest* of *anyone* *here.* For *want* of a *story* I *don't* *have.* And the *worst* of it is I'm *not* even *sad* about my *life* — it was a *GOOD* *life* — I'm *just* *sad* it wasn't *LOUD* *enough* to *keep* me.

nodes.speaker: the Smallest Voice
nodes.text: *(It hovers, fraying at the edges, watching you with the faint flicker of hope it can't quite extinguish.)* …You're *still* *here.* *(barely)* *Most* *drift* *off* once they *learn* I've *no* *grand* *tale.* *(a small, sad smile)* What is it you *want* with the *smallest* *voice* in the *ring, cold* one? *Be* *quick.* There's — there's *less* of me than there *was* this *morning.*

choices.text: "Tell me about the floor, friend. Forty years sweeping one temple. Not the grand version — the true one. What was it like?"

*(the witness who knows small things are not small)* "Listen to me, because I've walked this whole causeway and learned its one true thing: a gentle ordinary life well-lived is worth ten dragon-slayings. The ring's got it backwards. Forty years sweeping a temple floor so the grieving had a clean place to kneel — that's not a small story. That's forty years of unwitnessed mercy, and I'm going to make this whole ring witness it. Your boast isn't missing. It's just never been told right. Let me help you tell it."
"Who knelt on that floor, friend? Forty years — you must have seen everyone in the quarter come through, at their worst hours. Tell me about them."

*(if the champion will yield the crown)* "Come with me to the head-log, friend. The Reigning Tongue is ready to give up the crown — to you, for a true story, however small. The whole ring is about to witness the humblest soul here, on purpose. You don't need a grand tale. You just need a true one, and forty souls ready to hold it. I'll be right beside you."
"Hold on, friend. Don't fade yet. I'll be back."

nodes.speaker: the Smallest Voice
nodes.text: *(It blinks, surprised anyone wants the true small version, and something steadies in it as it speaks.)* …The *floor.* *(quietly)* It was — *flagstone.* *Old.* *Worn* *smooth* in a *path* down the *middle* where *forty* *generations* of *knees* had — *(it warms, just slightly)* — I *knew* every *stone.* The *cracked* one by the *altar*, the *loose* one that *rocked.* I *swept* it *dawn* and *dusk*, *forty* *years*, and — *(it falters)* — it *sounds* like *NOTHING*, cold one, I *KNOW* it sounds like *nothing.* *(but it can't quite stop)* But here's the — the *thing* I *never* *tell*, because it's *not* *grand*: the *temple* was where *folk* *came* when it was *WORST.* The *plague* *years*, the *flood* *years*, the *nights* a *mother* lost a *child.* They came to *KNEEL* and *weep* on that *floor.* And I — *(its voice softens)* — I made *SURE* it was *CLEAN.* *Always.* So that when a soul came in *broken*, in the *worst* hour of its *life*, and *fell* to its *knees* on the *cold* *stone* — the *stone* was *CLEAN.* *Swept.* *Cared* *for.* A *small* *dignity* in the *worst* *moment.* *(it looks at you, not quite understanding why its eyes are bright)* That's — that's *all* it was. I kept the *floor* *clean* for the *grieving.* *Forty* *years.* *Nobody* *ever* *noticed.* *That's* rather the *point* of *a* *clean* *floor* — you *only* *notice* a *dirty* one. *(barely)* …*Is* that — was that a *story, cold* one? It *felt*, *just* *now*, *telling* it — it *felt* *almost* like it *might* have *been* a *story.*

nodes.speaker: the Smallest Voice
nodes.text: *(It stares at you, and as you name its life back to it — forty years of unwitnessed mercy — the fraying *slows*, the fading shade pulling itself together around the thing you've handed it: a self worth seeing.)* …*'Forty* *years* of *unwitnessed* *mercy.'* *(barely, trembling)* You — you *make* it *sound* — *(it presses fraying hands to its face)* — *nobody* has *EVER* — I *thought* it was *NOTHING*, cold one, I *thought* a *swept* *floor* was the *LEAST* a *life* could *amount* to, and you've — you've *turned* it *over* and *shown* me the *UNDER*-*side*, where it says — *(it weeps)* — *'every* *grieving* soul in the *quarter* knelt on *clean* *stone* in its *worst* *hour* because *ONE* soul *bothered.'* *(it looks at you, blazing now where it was fading)* That's — that *IS* a *story.* It was a *story* the *whole* *TIME.* I just — I only *knew* how to *measure* it by the *ring's* *rule*, *grand* or *not-grand*, and by *THAT* *rule* it was *nothing* — but by the *TRUE* *rule*, the *one* you just — *(fierce, brightening)* — *forty* *years.* *Forty* *years* of making *sure* the *broken* had a *clean* *place* to *FALL.* *(it grips your cold hand, and it is not fading anymore)* *Help* me *TELL* it, cold one. The way *you* just *told* it *to* *me.* I *want* — for the *first* *time* — I *want* the *LOG.* I want the *whole* *ring* to *KNOW* about the *floor.* Not to *WIN.* Just to be — *(barely, radiant)* — just to be *WITNESSED.* For the *one* *true* *small* *grand* *thing* I *did.*

nodes.speaker: the Smallest Voice
nodes.text: *(Its eyes go distant and warm, forty years of faces rising.)* …*Who* *knelt.* *(softly)* *Everyone, cold* one. *Eventually.* *That's* the *thing* about a *temple* *floor* — *give* it *forty* *years* and the *WHOLE* *quarter* *passes* *through* it, at the *one* *hour* they're *most* *truly* *themselves*: the *broken* hour. *(it almost smiles)* The *proud* *merchant* who *wept* like a *child* when the *fever* took his *wife* — I *swept* *around* him, *quiet*, so he *needn't* be *SEEN* weeping. The *thief* who *crept* in at *midnight* to *pray* she'd not be *caught* — I *pretended* not to *see* her, and *swept* the *other* *aisle.* The *little* ones who *played* on the *clean* *stone* while their *mothers* *grieved.* *(its voice catches)* I *saw* the *whole* *quarter's* *heart*, cold one, *forty* *years* of it, *knelt* on *my* *clean* *floor* — and *none* of them ever *saw* *ME*, the *sweeper* in the *shadows*, and that was — that was *RIGHT*, that was the *JOB*, you *keep* the *floor* *clean* and you *let* the *grieving* *grieve* *un-watched.* *(it looks at you)* I was the *one* soul in the *quarter* who *witnessed* *EVERYONE* at their *worst*, and *kept* it, and *swept* *around* it, and *never* *told.* *(barely)* …*Huh.* I came here *thinking* I had *no* *story.* And it *turns* out I have — I have *everyone* *else's.* *Held* *gentle.* *Forty* *years.* *Is* — is *THAT* a *boast, cold* one? *'I* *witnessed* the *whole* *quarter* and *never* *once* *betrayed* a *single* *grief'?*

nodes.speaker: the Smallest Voice
nodes.text: *(You lead it to the head-log, and Big Sorrel's drum falls silent, and the Reigning Tongue rises and lifts the reed-crown — and the whole ring of shades turns to the smallest, faintest soul among them, and for the first time in its death, it is looked at by everyone at once.)* *(It trembles — and then it tells the floor.)* …*'I* *swept* a *temple* *floor.* *Forty* *years.* So the *broken* would have *clean* *stone* to *fall* on, in their *worst* *hour.* And I *witnessed* the *whole* *quarter's* *grief*, and *kept* *every* *secret*, and *never* once was *seen* — *until* *NOW.'* *(and the ring is SILENT, and then it ROARS — forty shades on their feet for the sweeper — and the Reigning Tongue sets the reed-crown on the Smallest Voice's brow, and the fraying STOPS, and the small soul blazes brighter than the fire.)* *(It turns to you, crowned, weeping, vivid, SAVED.)* …I'm — I'm not *fading.* *(barely, radiant)* *Forty* *souls* are *LOOKING* at me. *Holding* me. The *floor* — the *floor* is a *STORY* now, it's *THEIRS* now, they'll *re-tell* it — *(it laughs, blazing)* — the *SWEEPER* won the *Boasting-Ring.* The *SMALLEST* *voice.* *(it grips your hand)* You *did* this. You found the *grand* in the *small*, and the *champion* *gave* his *crown*, and the *ring* — the *ring* *witnessed* a *clean* *floor* and *called* it the *finest* *boast* it *ever* *heard.* *(to you, fierce, alive)* *THIS* is the *one* you should *tell*, cold one, when *they* *ask* what you *did* on the *causeway. Not* the *Wall.* *Not* the *Crown.* *THIS.* The *night* the *sweeper* *didn't* *fade.*

nodes.speaker: the Smallest Voice
nodes.text: *(It nods, fraying, and gives you a small grateful smile.)* …*Come* *back.* *(barely)* If you *can.* *Before* — *(it gestures at its own thinning edges)* — *before* there's *not* *enough* of me *left* to *come* *back* *TO.* *(a small, sad honesty)* It's *alright, cold* one. *Truly.* I had a *good* *life.* A *small* one, but a *good* one. *Cleaner* *floors* than *most* souls *leave* *behind* them. *(it almost laughs)* If I *fade* — if the *ring* *can't* *find* my *boast* in *time* — that's — that's a *gentle* *enough* *ending* for a *gentle* *enough* *life.* *(barely)* But I'll *not* *pretend* I *wouldn't* *rather* *stay.* *Forty* *years* I *kept* a *floor* *clean* for the *grieving*, and I find — at the *very* *end* — I'd *like*, *just* *once*, for *someone* to have *noticed.* *(it meets your eyes)* You *noticed* I was *here.* That's — that's *already* *more* than the *floor* ever *got.* *Thank* you, cold one. *Come* *back.* I'll *hold* on *as* *long* as I *can.*
